mechanism_narrative: Lower federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing present values of future earnings and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC for FEDFUNDS levels remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with rate declines.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rise in the unemployment rate above 5.5 percent signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer spending, which reduces corporate revenue expectations and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the following month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in > 65% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 5.5, or the signal out-of-sample hit rate falls below 50%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates broad-based domestic demand growth, boosting corporate earnings forecasts and generating positive equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive momentum in RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict positive 60-day returns with a Rank IC > 0.04, or the interaction term loses significance in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle reflects supply-side contraction that outweighs demand support, leading to margin compression and negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during tightening periods are followed by positive 5-day returns in > 60% of cases, or the regime-conditional signal shows no predictive power with p > 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in total nonfarm payrolls provides a stable baseline for consumer demand and corporate hiring, supporting equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows a negative or null correlation with 20-day forward returns across all tested windows, with Rank IC consistently below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price inflation exceeding 2.5 percent triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, which raises discount rates and compresses equity multiples, resulting in negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in > 70% of months where CPIAUCSL exceeds 238, or the threshold signal fails to beat a buy-and-hold benchmark.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between real GDP growth and personal consumption expenditures indicates structural demand weakness despite headline growth, reducing earnings visibility and driving negative equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The GDPC1-RSAFS spread shows no predictive power for 60-day returns, or positive spreads are followed by positive returns in > 65% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: A rising unemployment rate during a post-recession recovery phase indicates fragile labor market fundamentals that fail to sustain consumer spending, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: UNRATE increases during recovery phases are followed by positive 5-day returns in > 60% of cases, or the regime-specific signal shows no statistical edge.
